front: What is the definition of a connected graph?
back: A graph is connected if there is a path between every pair of vertices. If a graph is not connected, it is considered disconnected.

front: What is a cut vertex (or articulation point) in a graph?
back: A vertex v in a connected graph G is a cut vertex if the removal of v (and all edges incident to v) disconnects the graph, increasing the number of connected components.

front: What is the edge-connectivity (λ(G)) of a graph?
back: The edge-connectivity is the minimum number of edges whose removal disconnects the graph. It is the size of the smallest edge cut. A graph is k-edge-connected if its edge-connectivity is at least k.
For any graph G that is not complete, the vertex-connectivity κ(G) ≤ edge-connectivity λ(G) ≤ minimum degree δ(G). For a complete graph K_n, κ(K_n) = λ(K_n) = n-1.

front: What are the components of a disconnected graph?
back: The components are the maximal connected subgraphs. Each isolated vertex is also considered a component. A disconnected graph has at least two components.
